com.newland.nsdk.core.api.external.communication

Interface NSDKCommunicator

  • All Superinterfaces:
    ExternalCommunicator

    public interface NSDKCommunicator
extends ExternalCommunicator
    Default communicator provided by NSDK which used to establish communication channel.

    To get an ExternalCommunicator:

         NSDKCommunicator communicatorListener = new CommunicatorListener() {
         @Override
         public BluetoothDevice onBluetoothList(ArrayList arrayList) {
             // Choose expected bluetooth device to return.
         }

         @Override
         public void onConnectedStateChange(ExternalCommunicatorState externalCommunicatorState) {
             // Do something when state changes.
         }
     };
     NSDKCommunicator communicator = ExtNSDKModuleManagerImpl.getInstance()
                                             .getNSDKCommunicator(context, ExternalCommunicatorType.USB, communicatorListener);

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void close(int timeout)
      Closes communication channel.
      boolean isConnected()
      Checks if the communication channel is connected.
      void open(int timeout)
      Opens communication channel.

      • Methods inherited from interface com.newland.nsdk.core.api.external.communication.ExternalCommunicator

        clear, receive, send, setCommunicationTimeout

    • Method Detail

      • open

        void open(int timeout)
   throws NSDKException
        Opens communication channel. It shall be called before data exchange with external device.

        Note: To check if the external device is opened, call isConnected().

        Parameters:
        timeout - [Required] Timeout for opening. Unit: ms. Only used for bluetooth communicator now.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • close

        void close(int timeout)
    throws NSDKException
        Closes communication channel. It shall be called when data exchange finished.

        Note: To check if the external device is closed, call isConnected().

        Parameters:
        timeout - [Required] Timeout for closing. Unit: ms.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • isConnected

        boolean isConnected()
        Checks if the communication channel is connected.
        Returns:
        Communication channel state.
        • "true": The communication channel is connected.
        • "false": The communication channel is disconnected.
        Throws:
        NSDKException
